_meta.note: Competitor and market-landscape intelligence. Kisunla clinical facts are connector-grounded (see kisunla_profile.json). COMPETITOR clinical/regulatory facts are compiled from public disclosures and general knowledge and are NOT connector-grounded in this build - verify against primary sources (CLARITY-AD publication, FDA label, company filings) before any real decision. All market-size, share, and pricing figures are transparent, editable modelling assumptions, not asserted facts.

market_context.therapy_area: Early symptomatic Alzheimer's disease (MCI due to AD + mild AD dementia)
market_context.market_stage: Nascent, high-growth disease-modifying class (anti-amyloid mAbs); first meaningful DMTs after two decades of failures
market_context.growth_narrative: Class-level growth is gated less by demand than by diagnostic capacity, MRI/infusion infrastructure, and payer access (CMS CED). Uptake has been slower than analyst consensus.
key_market_drivers: Aging population and rising diagnosed-prevalence of early AD
Blood-based (plasma p-tau217) biomarkers lowering the diagnostic funnel cost
Traditional FDA approvals unlocking Medicare coverage under CED
key_market_barriers: ARIA monitoring burden and MRI access
APOE4 genotyping and risk counselling friction

market_sizing_assumptions_us._disclaimer: ILLUSTRATIVE modelling inputs for scenario analysis - edit in the app. Not market research findings.

competitors.modality: Humanized IgG1 mAb targeting amyloid-beta protofibrils
competitors.route: IV every 2 weeks (Q2W); subcutaneous autoinjector maintenance (IQLIK) FDA-approved 2025 for weekly maintenance
competitors.headline_efficacy: CDR-SB slowing ~27% vs placebo (difference approx -0.45); primary endpoint met
competitors.safety: ARIA-E ~12.6%; boxed warning for ARIA; serial MRI monitoring; higher risk in APOE4 homozygotes
competitors.strategic_notes: First-to-market advantage, entrenched registry/site relationships, and an approved SC maintenance option that reduces infusion burden - a direct threat to Kisunla's convenience story.

kisunla_vs_leqembi._disclaimer: Comparative framing for internal strategy. NOT a head-to-head trial; cross-trial comparisons are not statistically valid and must not be used promotionally.
kisunla_vs_leqembi.differentiation_thesis: Compete on total-cost-of-therapy and patient burden (finite dosing, fewer visits) rather than on a raw efficacy or safety claim; neutralise Leqembi's first-mover and SC-convenience advantages via value-based contracting and site enablement.

pipeline_and_disruptors: Subcutaneous / at-home anti-amyloid delivery lowering infusion dependence
Blood-based diagnostics (plasma p-tau217) collapsing the diagnostic funnel
Potential prevention/secondary-prevention indications expanding earlier populations
